response.justification: Wei Chen brings genuine geospatial experience through GISCollective and Green Map, and demonstrated ability to build high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, but the role's core requirement — modern C++ or Rust — is a critical gap. Their listed skills lead with TypeScript, DLang, and JavaScript; C++ appears only in a 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments writing IDE components and a bachelor thesis on wave field synthesis, neither of which constitutes the production-grade systems C++ this pipeline role demands. There is also no evidence of routing, navigation tile generation, or map data pipeline work, which are central to the position.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform development (GISCollective, Green Map GIS app) with real-world NGO and conservation use cases
Proven high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic: async I/O, low-latency DLang services handling millions of HTTP requests/second
Long-standing Linux fluency across server, DevOps, and embedded contexts throughout career
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust in any professional capacity; last C++ use was a 6-month role in 2014–2015 and a student thesis project
Zero experience with routing tiles, navigation pipelines, or production map data transformation
Current and recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js-first, with no signals of a transition toward systems-level languages
